title: Bar Chart — Sales Comparison
description: A company's monthly sales (in ₹ lakhs) are: Jan=45, Feb=52, Mar=48, Apr=60, May=55. What is the percentage increase from Jan to Apr?

title: Pie Chart — Budget Allocation
description: A family's monthly budget: Rent=35%, Food=25%, Transport=15%, Savings=15%, Others=10%. If total income is ₹60,000, how much is spent on Food and Transport combined?

title: Line Graph — Temperature Trend
description: Temperatures (°C) over 5 days: Mon=28, Tue=32, Wed=30, Thu=35, Fri=33. What is the average temperature?

title: Table — Production Analysis
description: Factory output (units): 2020=15000, 2021=18000, 2022=21600, 2023=25920. What is the consistent year-on-year growth rate?

title: Caselet — Profit Comparison
description: Company A earned ₹40 lakh profit in 2022 with a 25% margin. Company B earned ₹30 lakh with a 20% margin. Which company had higher revenue?
explanation: A's revenue = 40/0.25 = ₹160L. B's revenue = 30/0.20 = ₹150L. A has higher revenue.

title: Bar Chart — Average Calculation
description: Students in 5 sections: A=42, B=38, C=45, D=40, E=35. If the college wants average section strength of 42, how many more students are needed in total?

title: Pie Chart — Degree Calculation
description: In a pie chart, if 'Food' occupies 90°, what percentage of the total does food represent?

title: Mixed Chart — Revenue vs Expense
description: A company's data (₹ crores): Revenue=[100,120,110,130,140], Expenses=[80,95,90,100,105] for Years 2019-2023. In which year was the profit margin highest?

title: Table — Percentage Share
description: Export data ($ million): India=250, China=480, Japan=320, USA=550, Germany=400. What is India's percentage share of total exports?

title: Line Graph — Rate of Change
description: Population (thousands): 2018=200, 2019=220, 2020=253, 2021=278, 2022=320. Between which consecutive years was the growth rate highest?

title: Bar Chart — Ratio Comparison
description: Production (tonnes): Factory X=[80,90,100,110], Factory Y=[60,75,90,120] for Q1-Q4. In which quarter was X's production closest to Y's?
explanation: Differences: Q1=20, Q2=15, Q3=10, Q4=−10(absolute=10). Q3 and Q4 tied at 10. Q3 comes first.

title: Caselet — Exam Analysis
description: In an exam of 500 students: 60% passed in Math, 50% in English, and 30% passed in both. How many students failed in both subjects?
explanation: Passed at least one = 60+50−30 = 80%. Failed both = 20% of 500 = 100 students.

title: Stacked Bar — Component Analysis
description: Product sales (units) by region: North=[300,400], South=[200,350], East=[150,250], West=[350,300] for Year 1 and 2. Which region showed the highest growth?
explanation: Growth: N=33.3%, S=75%, E=66.7%, W=−14.3%. South grew the most at 75%.

title: Table — Per Capita Income
description: Country data: Population (million)=[50,120,80,200], GDP ($billion)=[500,960,720,1200]. Which country has the highest per capita income?

title: Pie Chart — Sector Wise Employment
description: Employment distribution: IT=25%, Manufacturing=30%, Agriculture=20%, Services=15%, Others=10%. If IT employs 50 lakh people, what is the total employment?

title: Table — CAGR Calculation
description: Revenue: Year 0=₹100 crore, Year 3=₹172.8 crore. What is the CAGR?

title: Bar Chart — Deviation from Average
description: Sales (₹000s): Store A=85, B=92, C=78, D=105, E=90. How much does Store D deviate from the average?

title: Line Graph — Intersection
description: Product A sales: 100,120,150,180,200 (Years 1-5). Product B: 200,190,170,160,150. In which year do they have approximately equal sales?
explanation: Year 3: A=150, B=170 (B>A). Year 4: A=180, B=160 (A>B). They cross between Year 3 and 4.

title: Table — Index Calculation
description: Base year (2015) price index = 100. Prices: 2016=108, 2017=115, 2018=125, 2019=140. What is the inflation rate from 2018 to 2019?

title: Caselet — Market Share
description: Total smartphone market is 80 million units. Brand A has 25% share, B has 20%, C has 15%. Rest is split equally among 4 other brands. How many units does each of the 4 brands sell?
explanation: A+B+C = 60%. Remaining = 40% of 80M = 32M. Each of 4 = 32/4 = 8 million

title: Mixed Data — Profit Analysis
description: Company data: Revenue=₹500 crore, COGS=₹300 crore, Operating Expenses=₹100 crore, Tax Rate=30%. What is the net profit?
explanation: Gross Profit = 500−300 = 200. Operating Profit = 200−100 = 100. Tax = 30% of 100 = 30. Net Profit = 100−30 = ₹70 crore

title: Table — Comparative Growth
description: Country GDP growth rates: India=[7.2,6.8,7.5,8.1], China=[6.5,6.2,5.8,5.5] for 2020-2023. What is India's average growth rate?

title: Pie Chart — Budget Reallocation
description: If a family reduces Food spend from 30% to 25% and adds it to Savings (originally 10%), and total budget is ₹1,00,000, what are the new Savings?

title: Bar Chart — Year-on-Year
description: Website visits (thousands): 2019=150, 2020=180, 2021=210, 2022=189, 2023=240. In which year did visits decline?

title: Table — Weighted Average
description: Three departments have scores: Dept A (50 employees, avg score 72), Dept B (30 employees, avg 85), Dept C (20 employees, avg 68). What is the overall weighted average?

title: Caselet — Loan EMI
description: A loan of ₹10 lakh at 12% annual interest simple for 5 years. What is the total amount to be repaid and the monthly installment (approx)?

title: Data Sufficiency — Sales
description:
Is the profit of Company X more than ₹50 crore?

I. Revenue is ₹200 crore.
II. Profit margin is 30%.
explanation: From I: Revenue=200. From II: Margin=30%. Together: Profit=200×30%=60>50. Both needed.

title: Table — Ratio Analysis
description: Company financials: Assets=₹500 crore, Liabilities=₹300 crore, Revenue=₹400 crore, Net Income=₹60 crore. What is the Debt-to-Equity ratio?

title: Line Graph — Forecast
description: Sales trend (₹ lakhs): Q1=100, Q2=115, Q3=132, Q4=152. If the same percentage growth continues, predict Q5.

title: Scatter Plot — Correlation
description: Data points show that as advertisement spending increases (₹10L,20L,30L,40L,50L), sales increase (₹100L,180L,250L,310L,360L). What is the approximate additional sales per ₹10L of ad spend?
explanation: Increments: 80,70,60,50. Average increment = (80+70+60+50)/4 = 65 lakhs per ₹10L spend.

title: Table — Break-Even Analysis
description: Fixed costs=₹5 lakhs/month, Variable cost=₹200/unit, Selling price=₹500/unit. How many units must be sold to break even?

title: Pie Chart — Multi-Year
description: 2022 Revenue=₹800 crore (IT=40%, Consulting=35%, Others=25%). 2023 Revenue=₹1000 crore (IT=45%, Consulting=30%, Others=25%). What is the absolute growth of IT segment?

title: Table — Rank Ordering
description: City-wise AQI (Air Quality Index): Delhi=285, Mumbai=142, Bangalore=78, Chennai=95, Kolkata=180. Rank them from best to worst air quality.
explanation: Lower AQI = better air. Order: Bangalore(78), Chennai(95), Mumbai(142), Kolkata(180), Delhi(285).

title: Bar Chart — Stacked Percentage
description: School exam results: Pass with distinction=15%, First class=35%, Second class=30%, Fail=20%. If 800 students appeared, how many got first class or above?

title: Table — Compound Metric
description: Employee data: Team A (10 people, 95% attendance, avg performance 8.2/10). Team B (15 people, 88% attendance, avg performance 8.7/10). Which team has higher effective productivity?
explanation: Effective score = attendance × performance. A: 0.95×8.2=7.79. B: 0.88×8.7=7.656. Team A is higher.

title: Mixed — Investment Returns
description: ₹1 lakh invested: 40% in stocks (return 15%), 30% in bonds (return 8%), 30% in FD (return 6%). What is the total return amount?

title: Table — Missing Value
description: A table shows: Input=10,Output=25; Input=20,Output=45; Input=30,Output=65; Input=40,Output=?. Find the pattern and the missing output.

title: Caselet — Election Results
description: Total votes=1,00,000. Candidate A got 45%, B got 35%, C got 15%, invalid=5%. By how many votes did A beat B?

title: Table — Efficiency Index
description: Machine output (units/hour): Machine A=120, B=95, C=150, D=110. Machine costs (₹/hour): A=600, B=400, C=900, D=500. Which machine has the best cost efficiency (units per rupee)?

title: Graph — Area Under Curve
description: A car's speed (km/h) over 4 hours: Hour 1=60, Hour 2=80, Hour 3=70, Hour 4=90. Approximately what total distance was covered?

title: Table — Standard Deviation Concept
description: Two students' scores in 5 tests: Student A=[80,82,78,81,79], Student B=[70,90,60,95,85]. Both average 80. Who is more consistent?
explanation: Student A's scores are all within 2 marks of the mean. Student B's scores vary widely (60 to 95). A has lower variance, hence more consistent.

title: Caselet — Population Density
description: City area=1500 sq km, Population=45 lakhs. Suburb area=3000 sq km, Population=30 lakhs. Compare population densities.
